groups_overview.title: Grouping Nodes
groups_overview.content:
Groups help you organize related nodes together. Think of them like folders for your audio devices!

When nodes are in a group:
• They're visually highlighted with the same color
• They move together when you drag one
• You can collapse them to reduce clutter

Perfect for keeping your setup tidy!
groups_overview.tip: Press G to open the Groups panel and see all your groups.

creating_groups.title: Creating a Group
creating_groups.content:
To create a group:

1. Select the nodes you want to group (Shift+click or box select)
2. Open the Groups panel (G)
3. Click 'Create Group from Selection'
4. Give your group a memorable name

Your nodes are now a team!
creating_groups.tip: Good group names describe the purpose: 'Streaming Setup', 'Music Production', 'Voice Chat'

group_colors.title: Group Colors
group_colors.content:
Each group gets its own color, making it easy to spot which nodes belong together at a glance.

Colors are assigned automatically, but the visual distinction really helps when you have multiple groups.
group_colors.tip: The colored border around nodes shows their group membership - no label needed!

moving_groups.title: Moving Groups Together
moving_groups.content:
Here's where groups really shine! When you drag a node that's in a group, all the other nodes in that group follow along.

This makes it easy to:
• Reorganize your layout without breaking groupings
• Keep related nodes positioned together
• Quickly make space for new additions
moving_groups.tip: Hold Ctrl while dragging to move just one node without its group buddies.

collapsing_groups.title: Collapsing Groups
collapsing_groups.content:
Groups can be collapsed to hide their members and reduce visual clutter. A collapsed group appears as a single compact node.

Great for:
• Hiding complex setups you don't need to see
• Focusing on what you're currently working on
• Making large graphs more manageable
collapsing_groups.tip: Collapsed groups still work normally - audio keeps flowing, you just don't see all the details.

managing_groups.title: Managing Groups
managing_groups.content:
From the Groups panel you can:

• See all your groups and their members
• Add more nodes to existing groups
• Remove nodes from groups
• Delete entire groups (nodes stay, just ungrouped)
• Expand or collapse group views
managing_groups.tip: Deleting a group doesn't delete the nodes - it just removes the organizational container.
